article_meta.title: ...By Natalie VenegasWeekend ReporterShareNewsweek is a Trust Project memberProtests have erupted across the country ove

reasoning: Mixed performance: several genuine author-prose biases were identified, but there was an attribution violation (flagging a quote) and multiple labels outside the approved taxonomy. Some minor author-level biases were missed.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: One clear violation: the model flagged Senator Padilla’s quoted phrase as bias, despite acknowledging it was a quote.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: At least one clear false positive (quoted text). Other items are largely defensible though a bit stretched in one case.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed additional author-prose slant such as 'brace for the impact of the president's crackdown' and other evaluative phrasing; otherwise caught main instances.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Used non-listed types ('Framing Bias', 'Selection Bias', 'Labeling Bias', 'Emotional Language') instead of the specified taxonomy (e.g., Word Choice, Slant, Bias by Omission, Sensationalism/Emotionalism).
